steps.title: Identify the key contrast signal
steps.content: The sentence pairs two siblings: “one sibling might inherit brown eyes; the other sibling, ______ might inherit green eyes.” The word “other” signals that we're contrasting different inherited traits.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
The logical relationship between the two clauses is contrast. One sibling inherits brown eyes; the "other sibling" inherits green. The word "other" explicitly flags the opposition, so the blank needs a contrast signal.
  • Choice A — for example,: signals that a specific instance of a general claim follows. But this clause is not an example of brown eyes — it's the contrasting opposite. ✗
  • Choice B — with this in mind,: signals that the second clause is a response to or consequence of the first. Inheriting green eyes is not a consequence of inheriting brown eyes. ✗
  • Choice C — lastly,: signals the final item in a sequence. No prior list has been set up for a final entry. ✗
  • Choice D — by contrast,: signals direct opposition between the two clauses, fitting perfectly with the one sibling vs. the other structure. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
